text: This is the VOA Special English Technology Report.
zh: [0] 这里是美国之音特别英语科技报道。

text: Last week, we told you about the Imagine Cup Finals in the United States.
zh: [1] 上周，我们向大家介绍了在美国举行的“畅想杯”决赛。

text: Team Flash Food from Arizona State University won top honors at the Microsoft event.
zh: [2] 来自亚利桑那州立大学的“闪电食物”团队在这场微软举办的赛事中获得了最高荣誉。

text: The team developed a mobile application and website to help restaurants, hotels,
zh: [3] 该团队开发了一款手机应用和网站，帮助餐馆、酒店，

text: and farmers markets donate food to people who need it.
zh: [4] 以及农贸市场将食物捐赠给有需要的人。

text: The businesses enter information about the food they would like to donate.
zh: [5] 商家可以输入他们想要捐赠的食物信息。

text: A notification goes to a team of volunteer drivers who can go pick up the leftover food
zh: [6] 系统会向志愿司机团队发送通知，他们可以前去取走剩余的食物，

text: and take it to people in need. This is perishable food,
zh: [7] 并送给有需要的人。这些是易腐食品，

text: the kind of food that could not normally be handled by food banks or other food recovery organizations.
zh: [8] 是那种通常食物银行或其他食物回收机构无法处理的食品。

text: The FLASH Food Project grew out of the Engineering Projects in Community Service Program
zh: [9] “闪电食物”项目源自亚利桑那州立大学的

text: at Arizona State. The director of that program, Richard Philly,
zh: [10] “社区服务工程项目”计划。该计划的负责人理查德·菲利，

text: says the project has far -reaching possibilities. Mr. Philly plans to work with the team
zh: [11] 表示这个项目具有深远的发展潜力。菲利先生计划与团队合作，

text: to find ways to extend the Flash Food app to other countries.
zh: [12] 寻找将“闪电食物”应用推广到其他国家的方法。

text: In his words, would those cultures in those different countries be open
zh: [13] 用他的话说，那些不同国家的文化

text: to the idea of taking leftover food and using flash food to connect with people who are hungry?
zh: [14] 是否愿意接受用剩余食物、通过“闪电食物”来联系饥饿人群这一理念呢？

text: We don't know the answer to that yet, says Mr. Philly.
zh: [15] 菲利先生说，目前我们还不知道答案。

text: The Imagine Cup is now in its 10th year.
zh: [16] “畅想杯”如今已经举办到第十届。

text: Mark Hinsboe is a vice president with Microsoft.
zh: [17] 马克·辛斯博是微软的一位副总裁。

text: He says students who enter the competition can now build their ideas around a lot of technology
zh: [18] 他说，如今参赛的学生们可以借助许多技术来实现自己的创意，

text: that was not widely available ten years ago.
zh: [19] 而这些技术在十年前还并不普及。

text: In the beginning, he says, people were building almost everything from scratch.
zh: [20] 他说，一开始，大家几乎都是从零开始搭建一切。

text: But now, he points to the Flash Food app
zh: [21] 但现在，他指出“闪电食物”应用

text: as a good example of bringing together existing technologies.
zh: [22] 就是整合现有技术的一个很好的例子。

text: The students built the app on the assumption that there are smart phones
zh: [23] 学生们在开发这款应用时假设

text: in the hands of most of the people they need to work with.
zh: [24] 他们需要合作的大多数人手中都有智能手机。

text: Also, the use of mapping software lets the app give the volunteer drivers
zh: [25] 此外，地图软件的使用让这款应用能够

text: turn -by -turn directions to the restaurants with food to donate.
zh: [26] 为志愿司机提供前往捐赠食物餐馆的逐步导航路线。

text: Team Flash Food will represent the United States
zh: [27] “闪电食物”团队将代表美国

text: at the Imagine Cup World Finals in Australia in
zh: [28] 参加7月在澳大利亚举行的

text: July. Mark Hinsboe says says solving world problems like hunger
zh: [29] “畅想杯”世界总决赛。马克·辛斯博表示，解决像饥饿这样的世界性问题

text: is a major goal of the competition. But another major goal is to get more American students
zh: [30] 是这项比赛的主要目标之一。但另一个重要目标是让更多美国学生

text: interested in jobs in science and engineering. When you look at the pipeline in
zh: [31] 对科学和工程类工作产生兴趣。他说，当你审视科学、技术、

text: science, technology, engineering, and math, we come up way short, he says.
zh: [32] 工程和数学人才的储备时，会发现我们远远不足。

text: As a result, he says around a million software and tech jobs could go unfilled in the United
zh: [33] 因此，他说，在未来五年里，美国可能会有

text: States over the next five years.
zh: [34] 大约一百万个软件和科技岗位无人填补。

text: For VOA Special English, I'm Carolyn Presutti.
zh: [35] 我是卡罗琳·普雷苏蒂，为您带来美国之音特别英语报道。
